headline: The FBI and CISA warn that Russian intelligence now steals Signal Backup Recovery Keys to read victims' entire encrypted message history.

emerging_trends.text: Russian intelligence keeps winning with low-tech deception, now coaxing Signal users into surrendering their Backup Recovery Keys, proof that a human handoff defeats end-to-end encryption.
emerging_trends.topic_title: Russia Targets Signal Backup Recovery Keys

emerging_trends.text: Enterprises rushing autonomous AI agents into production are scrambling to govern the machine identities those agents inherit, the bet behind Cisco's purchase of non-human-identity startups Astrix and WideField.
emerging_trends.topic_title: AI Agent Identity Becomes the New Control Plane

emerging_trends.text: The shrinking window between disclosure and exploitation is pushing the open-source world toward funded remediation, the rationale for the Linux Foundation's new Akrites initiative.
emerging_trends.topic_title: Linux Foundation Launches Akrites for Open Source

topics.title: Russia Targets Signal Backup Recovery Keys
topics.latest_developments: The FBI and CISA updated their March 2026 advisory on June 26 to warn that Russian intelligence phishing now tricks Signal users into handing over their Backup Recovery Key, which restores the full message history and keeps working after the theft, while Ukraine's SBU detailed the fake tech-support crews running the operation.
topics.summary: Russian Intelligence Services phish users of Signal, WhatsApp, and Telegram by posing as tech support to harvest credentials and link rogue devices; reject unsolicited device-linking and recovery-key requests.

sources.title: FBI: Russian hackers now target Signal backup recovery keys
FBI Warns Russian Intelligence Hackers Target Signal Backup Recovery Keys
Russian Intelligence Services Continue to Target Commercial Messaging Applications
Russia used social engineering to breach prominent messaging accounts, Ukraine says

topics.title: AI Agent Identity Becomes the New Control Plane
topics.latest_developments: Cisco acquired non-human-identity startups Astrix and WideField to make identity the control plane for autonomous AI agents, Proof launched x401, an open protocol for verifying the identity behind agents, and BleepingComputer flagged threat actors spinning up fraudulent OpenAI organization tenants to phish company secrets from employees they invite.
topics.summary: AI agents inherit human permissions and act at machine speed, outpacing identity governance designed for people; vendors are racing to bolt on non-human-identity controls as attackers exploit the same agentic tools.

sources.title: Cisco Adds NHI to Security Stack With Astrix, WideField Acquisitions
Proof's x401 establishes an open protocol for AI agent identity and authorization
Cybersecurity firms targeted by fraudulent OpenAI organization invites
Guardian Agents: The Next Layer of Identity Governance

topics.title: Linux Foundation Launches Akrites for Open Source
topics.latest_developments: The Linux Foundation launched Akrites on June 26, an industry initiative joining technology firms, banks, security vendors, and AI companies to report, patch, and disclose vulnerabilities in widely used open-source software, while a separate Open Source Sustainability Initiative took aim at securing end-of-life projects.
topics.summary: Both efforts respond to AI shortening the gap between flaw discovery and exploitation, giving maintainers funded channels for coordinated disclosure and giving enterprises a path to manage aging, out-of-support code.

sources.title: Critical open-source projects get a new security framework
Linux Foundation Unveils New Open Source Security Project Akrites
New Initiative Tackles Security for End-of-Life Open Source Software

topics.title: Linux Kernel Root Exploits: pedit COW and DirtyClone
topics.latest_developments: JFrog Security Research published a working DirtyClone exploit on June 25 for CVE-2026-43503 in the DirtyFrag family, and a public exploit for pedit COW, CVE-2026-46331 in the act_pedit traffic-control action, appeared within a day of its June 16 assignment.
topics.summary: Both flaws let a local unprivileged Linux user corrupt shared page-cache memory and gain root; Red Hat rates them high severity and patches have landed upstream, so administrators should update kernels now.

sources.title: New Linux pedit COW Exploit Enables Root Access by Poisoning Cached Binaries
New DirtyClone Linux Kernel Flaw Lets Local Users Gain Root via Cloned Packets

topics.title: Passport and Pentagon Data Exposures
topics.latest_developments: A database of nearly one million passports from around the world leaked after attackers hit an age-verification system used by cannabis dispensaries, and the Pentagon opened an inquiry into the Dialog data exposure that unmasked a senior White House intelligence official and an active-duty special operations officer.
topics.summary: Both incidents show high-value credentials and identities spilling through low-value ancillary systems; affected people face heightened identity-theft and targeting risk.

sources.title: One Million Passports Leaked Online
The Pentagon Is Looking Into the Dialog Data Exposure for Unmasking National Security Officials

topics.title: CISA Orders Urgent Cisco Unified CM Patch
topics.latest_developments: CISA gave federal agencies until Sunday, June 28, to patch CVE-2026-20230, a server-side request forgery flaw in Cisco Unified Communications Manager that attackers are actively exploiting through automated Tor sweeps dropping webshells.
topics.summary: The high-severity bug opens a file-write path to root on Cisco's enterprise voice platform; Cisco has shipped fixes, and all operators should apply them immediately.

sources.title: CISA sets urgent deadline to fix Cisco flaw exploited in attacks

weather: Tonight: Showers And Thunderstorms Likely then Patchy Fog, low 66F.
Sunday: Partly Sunny then Chance Showers And Thunderstorms, high 85F.

games.recap: Marte's tiebreaking homer in 8th after 4-run inning against Skenes helps Reds beat Pirates 6-4

business_politics.title: U.S. Strikes on Iran Push Oil Back Above $70
business_politics.latest_developments: Oil futures climbed back above $70 a barrel and the S&P 500 logged its fifth straight losing session on June 26 as Washington confirmed the fresh strikes, leaving the ceasefire between the two adversaries in doubt.
business_politics.summary: The United States launched military strikes on Iran after a drone attack on a cargo ship in the Strait of Hormuz that President Trump called a ceasefire violation, reviving fears of a wider Middle East conflict and an energy-supply shock.

sources.title: U.S. Launches Fresh Attacks on Iran
US launches strikes on Iran after Tehran attacks container ship
Oil Prices Rise Above $70 on Iran Strike

business_politics.title: Trump Threatens 100% Tariff Over Digital Services Taxes
business_politics.latest_developments: Trump warned on June 26 that he would impose a 100% tariff on any country that implements a digital services tax, saying the levy would supersede existing trade deals.
business_politics.summary: The threat targets nations, including European states, that tax U.S. technology giants' digital revenue, sharpening trade tensions as the International Monetary Fund's departing chief economist, Pierre-Olivier Gourinchas, warned that tit-for-tat trade warfare is self-defeating and threatens the global economy.

sources.title: Trump warns of 100% tariff on countries implementing digital services tax
'Tit-for-tat' trade warfare threatens global economy, says IMF chief economist

business.title: EmployHer Job Fair Draws Women in Homestead
business.latest_developments: Dress For Success Pittsburgh, When She Thrives, and partners held the EmployHer job fair in Homestead this week to help women change industries or start careers.
business.summary: EmployHer, a collaboration led by Dress For Success Pittsburgh and its director of community engagement, Liz Mims, ran a Homestead job fair connecting women with employers across every career stage.

sources.title: Pittsburgh organizations aim to support women during all stages of their careers

business.title: Cambria County's Starfire Powers Pittsburgh's July 4
business.latest_developments: The Post-Gazette profiled Starfire, the Cambria County pyrotechnics firm, on June 27 ahead of the Independence Day fireworks show over Pittsburgh.
business.summary: Starfire, a family-run fireworks company based in Cambria County, designs and fires Pittsburgh's July 4 display, with its operators describing themselves as 'a very noisy group.'

sources.title: 'We're a very noisy group': Pittsburgh's July 4 fireworks are powered by the artistry of Cambria County's Starfire

around_town.title: Justice Department Sues Pennsylvania Over SNAP Data
around_town.latest_developments: The Justice Department announced late June 26 it is suing Pennsylvania and three other states to force them to hand over food-stamp applicant data.
around_town.summary: The federal suit demands Supplemental Nutrition Assistance Program applicant records that Pennsylvania and three other states have refused to provide, setting up a fight over data privacy and federal authority.

sources.title: Justice Department sues Pa., other states for refusing to provide SNAP applicant data

around_town.title: Lawsuit Says Title X Rule Imperils Pa. Reproductive Care
around_town.latest_developments: A lawsuit challenges a Trump administration rule that plaintiffs say puts reproductive health care for 160,000 Pennsylvania patients at risk, the Post-Gazette reported June 27.
around_town.summary: The suit targets a federal Title X family-planning rule that the complaint warns would cut off reproductive health services for roughly 160,000 patients across Pennsylvania.

sources.title: Trump admin rule puts reproductive health care for 160K Pa. patients at risk, lawsuit says

around_town.title: Woodland Hills Superintendent Firing Hearing Turns Tense
around_town.latest_developments: Cross-examination grew tense June 26 in the fifth and final hearing session over the Woodland Hills school board's vote to fire Superintendent Joe Maluchnik.
around_town.summary: Superintendent Joe Maluchnik, accused of gender-based discrimination and harassment, is appealing the Woodland Hills school board's decision to fire him in a courtroom-style hearing held across five nights.

sources.title: Cross-examination turns tense in final hearing session over firing of Woodland Hills superintendent

events.title: America250PA Concert at Point State Park
events.latest_developments: Crews closed Point State Park on June 26 to set up for the June 27 Commonwealth Concert, with rapper Frzy opening for headliners Nelly and Third Eye Blind.
events.summary: The America250PA Commonwealth Concert takes over Point State Park in downtown Pittsburgh on Saturday, June 27, with Frzy opening ahead of headliners Nelly and Third Eye Blind for the nation's 250th-anniversary celebration.

sources.title: Preparations underway for America250PA concert on Saturday
Pittsburgh-area things to do this weekend: Warhol Bridge turns 100, Frzy opens concert at the Point, more

events.title: South Side Street Fest's Second Weekend
events.latest_developments: Organizers added metal detectors, ID scanners, and a dedicated line for locals for the festival's June 26-27 return after long entry lines marred opening weekend.
events.summary: The South Side Street Fest runs a second weekend along the South Side on Friday and Saturday, June 26 and 27, with new metal detectors and ID scanners to speed entry; organizers expect Saturday's larger crowds to test the changes.

sources.title: South Side Street Fest adds more metal detectors and ID scanner to speed up lines

around_teams.title: Pirates Celebrate Don Kelly's 100th Win
around_teams.latest_developments: The Post-Gazette reported June 26 that Pirates manager Don Kelly notched his 100th career managerial win, with players saying 'he deserves it.'
around_teams.summary: Pittsburgh Pirates manager Don Kelly reached 100 career wins, a milestone the clubhouse celebrated while hoping a postseason push follows.

sources.title: 'He deserves it': The Pirates celebrate Don Kelly's 100th win — and hope more are on the way

around_teams.title: Pirates Activate Konnor Griffin From the IL
around_teams.latest_developments: The Pirates activated top prospect Konnor Griffin from the injured list on June 26, adding reinforcement to an injury-thinned roster.
around_teams.summary: Pittsburgh brought prospect Konnor Griffin back from the injured list, a needed lift for a Pirates lineup depleted by injuries to first baseman Spencer Horwitz and others.

sources.title: Pirates activate Konnor Griffin from the IL, providing much-needed reinforcements

around_teams.title: Eric Ebron Joins 'Not Just Football'
around_teams.latest_developments: Former tight end Eric Ebron appeared on Cam Heyward's 'Not Just Football' on June 24, discussing Andrew Luck's retirement, the 11-0 Steelers team, and his own retirement at 28.
around_teams.summary: On Cam Heyward's 'Not Just Football' podcast, ex-Steeler Eric Ebron shared locker-room stories from his stops in Detroit, Indianapolis, and Pittsburgh and reflected on walking away from the game at 28.

sources.source: Not Just Football with Cam Heyward
sources.title: Eric Ebron Talks Andrew Luck's Retirement, The 11-0 Steelers Team, & More! | Not Just Football

team_usa.title: USMNT Falls to Türkiye Before Round of 32
team_usa.latest_developments: The United States, already through as Group D winner, lost 3-2 to Türkiye in its group finale, and Christian Pulisic returned from a calf injury to a bright start, ESPN reported June 27.
team_usa.summary: Mauricio Pochettino's second-string United States lost 3-2 to Türkiye in a dead-rubber group finale that offered lessons on his backups before the World Cup round of 32 on July 1; Christian Pulisic, back from a left-calf injury, started sharply before the late collapse.

sources.title: USMNT must heed World Cup lessons learned in loss to Türkiye
Pulisic looks ready to make his mark on USMNT's World Cup

reading.author: Ed Zitron
reading.title: Premium: Notes From The Bubble, Volume 1
reading.summary: Zitron launches a running series of dispatches chronicling what he sees as the inflating AI and tech bubble, tracking the industry's strained finances and hype in real time.

reading.author: Stratechery
reading.title: An Interview with Figma CEO Dylan Field About Design and AI
reading.summary: Ben Thompson interviews Figma chief executive Dylan Field on building the company and why Field believes AI gives the design platform a tailwind rather than a threat.

reading.author: Cal Newport
reading.title: Dear AI Companies: Stop the "Doom Trolling"
reading.summary: Newport argues that AI companies erode public trust by publishing alarmist warnings about the dangers of their own products, a self-defeating posture he likens to a carmaker fear-mongering over its bestselling truck.

sports_media: Not Just Football with Cam Heyward
